perturbation.original_text: You (which term shall include your subsidiaries or other entities controlled by you) have requested information regarding Zeigler Coal Holding Company (which term, together with its subsidiaries or other controlled entities, the "Company", "us" or "we") in connection with your consideration of the possible acquisition of the Company (a "Possible Transaction").
perturbation.changed_text: You (which term shall include your subsidiaries or other entities controlled by you) have requested information regarding Zeigler Coal Holding Company (which term, together with its subsidiaries, the "Company", "us" or "we") in connection with your consideration of the possible acquisition of the Zeigler Coal Holding Company (a "Possible Venture").
perturbation.explanation: The term "Possible Transaction" is changed to "Possible Venture" in the context of the acquisition. However, throughout the document, the original term "Possible Transaction" is used. This creates a contradiction as to whether 'Possible Transaction' and 'Possible Venture' mean the same thing. This difference affects the obligations related to the acquisition process. The contradiction appears in the subsequent references to the acquisition throughout the document. It creates uncertainty about which term is the correct one to use, thereby affecting the enforcement of clauses referring to the acquisition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You (which term shall include your subsidiaries or other entities controlled by you) have requested information regarding Zeigler Coal Holding Company (which term, together with its subsidiaries, the "Company", "us" or "we") in connection with your consideration of the possible acquisition of the Zeigler Coal Holding Company (a "Possible Venture"). In consideration of our furnishing you with the Evaluation Materials (as defined below) you agree as follows: CONFIDENTIALITY OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either we or our financial advisor, CREDIT SUISSE FIRST BOSTON CORPORATION ("CSFB"), or our other representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials").
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the acquisition as a "Possible Transaction". The changed text redefines the acquisition within the same sentence as a "Possible Venture". The contradicted text references "Possible Venture", which the explanation says is problematic because the document subsequently refers to the acquisition as "Possible Transaction". Since the change alters the established terminology (Possible Transaction) to 'Possible Venture' while the document continues to use the former, a strong in-text contradiction is created, making the contract ambiguous and difficult to enforce. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either we or our financial advisor, CREDIT SUISSE FIRST BOSTON CORPORATION ("CSFB"), or our other representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials").
perturbation.changed_text: You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either we or our financial advisor, GOLDMAN SACHS & CO. ("GS"), or our other representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials").
perturbation.explanation: Changing the financial advisor from "CREDIT SUISSE FIRST BOSTON CORPORATION ("CSFB")" to "GOLDMAN SACHS & CO. ("GS")" creates a conflict later in the document when the original advisor is referenced. This creates ambiguity as to who the actual financial advisor is and who should be contacted for inquiries. The contradiction affects the section regarding 'NO UNAUTHORIZED CONTACT OR SOLICITATION' section later in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the course of your evaluation, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to CSFB or employees or representatives of the Company specified by CSFB. Accordingly, you agree not to directly or indirectly contact or communicate with any executive or other employee of the Company concerning is Possible Transaction, or to seek any information in connection therewith from such person, without the express consent of CSFB. You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party without CSFB's prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies CREDIT SUISSE FIRST BOSTON CORPORATION ("CSFB") as the financial advisor, while the changed text replaces it with GOLDMAN SACHS & CO. ("GS"). The contradicted text states that all inquiries and communications should be made directly to CSFB. Since the financial advisor is now GS, this directly contradicts the instruction to contact CSFB, creating a clear inconsistency as it is now unclear to whom inquiries should be directed, thereby fulfilling the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: You recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection,and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential."
perturbation.changed_text: You recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes only information furnished to you in writing (whatever the form or storage medium).
perturbation.explanation: By limiting 'Evaluation Materials' to only written information, the agreement now contradicts itself because it originally included information furnished orally. This affects the confidentiality obligations related to oral information. This leads to an in-text contradiction since the original definition included 'oral' information. This change impacts the reach of the confidentiality clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either we or our financial advisor, GOLDMAN SACHS & CO. ("GS"), or our other representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). You recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes only information furnished to you in writing (whatever the form or storage medium).
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Evaluation Materials' as including both oral and written information. The changed text narrows this definition to include only written information. The contradicted text initially states 'You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral)...' and then includes the modified definition. This creates a direct contradiction because the first sentence of the contradicted text includes oral information while the modified definition excludes it, creating an inconsistency in what is considered 'Evaluation Materials' and therefore, what must be treated confidentially.

perturbation.original_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose, including evaluating a Potential Investment.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Possible Transaction' to 'Potential Investment' while keeping the definition of 'Evaluation Materials' linked to the former creates a conflict. The original definition is supposed to cover ONLY what is in respect to 'Possible Transaction'. This creates confusion and in-text contradiction, because 'Evaluation Material' is not defined relative to the new term, creating enforcement and interpretation issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose, including evaluating a Potential Investment. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, <PAGE> representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you, and shall each expressly agree, to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents by writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the use of evaluation materials to 'Possible Transaction'. The changed text expands this restriction to include 'Potential Investment' but the definition of 'Evaluation Materials' remains tied to 'Possible Transaction' as defined in the explanation. The contradicted text repeats the restriction now referring to 'Potential Investment', but the scope of 'Evaluation Materials' is still defined by 'Possible Transaction', creating a contradiction because the Evaluation Materials themselves are only defined relative to 'Possible Transaction' while the text now restricts use for a 'Potential Investment', for which no 'Evaluation Materials' are defined.

perturbation.original_text: NON-DISCLOSURES The disclosure of your possible interest in purchasing the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: NON-DISCLOSURES The disclosure of your possible interest in purchasing the Corporation could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The change of the 'Company' for 'Corporation' create the interpretation issue of which organization are they actually referring to. Given this is a binding agreement this could create enforment issues. The In-Text Contradiction appears because there is an instance were the document uses Company instead of Corporation in other parts of the document
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURES The disclosure of your possible interest in purchasing the Corporation could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof. The term "person" as used in this letter shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporation, the Company, governmental agency or body, stock exchange, partnership, association or individual.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Company' to 'Corporation' introduces misaligned terminology, a form of in-text contradiction. The explanation highlights that this change creates ambiguity about which entity is being referenced, potentially leading to enforcement issues. The contradicted text, even if it's a restatement of the changed text, reinforces that the document uses 'Company' and 'Corporation' and this inconsistency constitutes a contradiction because it could allow a party to argue that an obligation related to one does not apply to the other.
